page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving top-tier results in both cached and uncached read/write operations, including 480.96 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, software compilation with Clang, and Redis SET operations, where it handles 199.17 million operations per second. Single-core performance is moderately strong, with Geekbench single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling in the top 25%.

The c8gn.24xlarge offers high resource density for compute-heavy workloads, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for parallel processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth supports high-throughput data transfers. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive applications. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and features a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the c8gn.24xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database and Redis operations. While single-core performance is moderately strong, the server excels in highly parallelized environments. It is best suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database management, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.

tagline: A high-core ARM64 compute platform delivering exceptional multi-threaded performance and high-speed networking for parallelized enterprise workloads.

192 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Optimized for software compilation, Redis, and static web serving.
